page: Microsoft Azure D16s_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supported by a 48 MB L3 cache, 1 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. This instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include bundled local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable, allowing users to attach external high-performance persistent disks.

In benchmark evaluations, the D16s_v5 demonstrates consistent mid-tier performance. It achieved a Geekbench single-core score of 1625.0 and a multi-core score of 9869.0, alongside a PassMark CPU score of 21254.08. Memory bandwidth testing shows a read-only small block cached speed of 833.67 GB/sec and an uncached large block read speed of 99.68 GB/sec. For specialized workloads, the server processed Redis SET operations at 15.61 million operations per second and delivered a static web serving throughput of 21.23 GB/sec. Notably, its text generation speed for a small 135M parameter LLM model reached 285.04 tokens per second, placing it in the top 25% tier, while its prompt processing speed for the same model averaged 1367.23 tokens per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D16s_v5 offers a standard resource density suitable for general-purpose workloads without the premium cost of specialized memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware. However, the lack of bundled local storage means that storage costs are externalized to attached network volumes, which represents a key architectural tradeoff. The instance is suited for workloads such as software compilation, database operations, static web serving, and lightweight language model inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D16s_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DSv5 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 instance features 8 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz with a 48 MB L3 cache. It lacks bundled local storage and GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Benchmarks indicate solid mid-range performance, with a PassMark CPU score of 21254.08, a static web serving throughput of 21.23 GB/sec, and a strong text generation speed of 285.04 tokens per second for small LLM models. The balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it cost-effective for general-purpose applications, software compilation, database operations, and web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D16s_v5 is a general-purpose VM featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and Premium Storage support, delivering balanced performance for standard enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D16s_v5 is a general-purpose VM with 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and Premium Storage support, optimized for balanced workloads.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 virtual machine featuring sixteen dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and sixty-four gigabytes of memory for general-purpose workloads.

64.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB RAM per vCPU ratio
Premium Storage capable configuration with no bundled local storage

Suitable for software compilation, database operations, and static web serving
